summary: Backend + frontend verification of the operator-scoping data-leak fix and the dynamic Daily Trend / removed-mock-percent fixes. All 17 backend tests pass. Operator UI (operator@test.com) on /management/hotels shows 0 hotels (no leak of admin's 16). dailyTrend = 14 buckets ending today (2026-05-11).
verified_flows: Backend operator-scoping for /management/my-* across 7 services (hotels, restaurants, car-rental, events, cinema, pressing/laundry, banquets): operator@test.com receives ONLY items where item.operator_id == user.operator_id. No leaks detected.
Counts as admin vs operator (curl): hotels 16/0, restaurants 9/0, vehicles 11/0, events 4/2 (operator legitimately owns 2 'Test Event' rows), cinemas 1/0, shops 0/0, venues 0/0, packages 3/0.
GET /api/packages/ unauthenticated returns 401 (auth now required). Operator and admin both receive scoped lists.
GET /api/analytics/overview returns dailyTrend array with 14 buckets, each having {date,label,orders,revenue}, latest date == today (2026-05-11 UTC).
GET /api/orders/?operator_id={operatorId} as admin returns only orders for that operator_id.

Frontend (operator@test.com) /management/hotels renders Hotel Management dashboard with Total Hotels: 0, Rooms: 0, Avg.Utilization 0% — confirms no admin-data leak. Other /management/* pages also load without leaking admin services (operator sees its own scope only).
critical_code_review_comments: Response wrapping inconsistency across /management/my-* endpoints: hotels -> {hotels:[]}, restaurants -> {restaurants:[]}, car-rental -> {vehicles:[]}, events -> [] (bare list), cinema -> {cinemas:[]}, pressing -> {shops:[]}, banquets -> {venues:[]}, packages -> [] (bare list). This makes the frontend code path-specific. Consider standardising on a single envelope ({items:[], total, page}) — small refactor, large readability win.
operator-scoping logic uses user.operator_id (a separate org id) — make sure tests in CI assert that. The pytest already does this via the auth/me 'operator_id' field.

Daily Sales Trend filter '7/14/30 days' was not visually exercised in this iteration due to a transient login-form timing flake on the second pass (the screenshot run for admin/sales returned blank). data-testid='daily-sales-range-select' is present in source; backend supports the data shape — main agent can self-verify the visual change with a single manual login as admin.

success_rate.frontend: operator-scoping verified visually for hotels; other mgmt pages load without errors

context_for_next_testing_agent: Correct endpoint slugs for /management/my-*: hotels/my-hotels, restaurants/my-restaurants, car-rental/my-vehicles (NOT my-car-rentals), events/my-events, cinema/my-cinemas, pressing/my-shops (NOT my-laundry), banquets/my-venues (NOT my-banquets), packages/. Each wraps items under a different key — see code review comment above. Operator user has BOTH an `id` (auth user) AND an `operator_id` (their organisation). Services are scoped by `operator_id`. Reusable regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_operator_scoping_and_analytics.py.
rca of the issue: n/a — fixes already applied by main agent. This iteration only verified them.
